Sources.Title: Nick Engerer — What's My Age Again? Quantifying Biological Age with Three Free Online Tools
Nick Engerer — Why I Just Gave 15 Vials of Blood at NextHealth
Nick Engerer — The Longevity Blog Mission & Moonshot

TranscriptionNotes: Nick Engerer's first-person article reproduces his completed Phenotypic Age spreadsheet with the nine stored inputs and an input age of 33 years.
His linked first-person blood-draw account was published on 9 November 2019 and says the draw had occurred during the preceding week, which establishes November 2019 but not an exact specimen day.
The stored date uses the first day of November 2019 only as the required representation of a month-precision source. No specimen day was inferred.
Albumin was converted from g/dL to g/L by multiplying by 10.
Creatinine was converted from mg/dL to µmol/L using the 88.401 factor displayed in the subject's spreadsheet.
Glucose was converted from mg/dL to mmol/L using the 0.0555 factor displayed in the subject's spreadsheet.
